deep_dive.title: Switch-Reasoner: Learn When to Think in Multitask Mixtures via Reinforcement Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) often follow a fixed Think-then-Answer paradigm, which is inefficient in heterogeneous multitask settings because simple inputs may not require explicit reasoning while difficult ones can benefit substantially from it. Learning when to think is also unstable during post-training, where imbalanced rollouts can drive the model toward always-thinking or always-direct behavior. We propose Switch-Reasoner, a GRPO-based framework that learns to adaptively select reasoning modes for MLLMs. It treats thinking as a virtual tool invocation and allows the model to either answer directly or invoke explicit reasoning before answering. To stabilize this decision, we introduce a dual-level regulation mechanism that balances the overall use of Thinking Mode and Direct Mode while providing sample-level supervision based on the relative benefit of the two choices. Experiments on 11 multimodal tasks show that Switch-Reasoner reduces unnecessary reasoning while maintaining strong performance, achieving a better accuracy-efficiency trade-off.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: GRPO-based framework to adaptively select reasoning modes for MLLMs via reinforcement learning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于GRPO的强化学习框架，让多模态大模型自适应选择推理模式
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于GRPO的强化学习框架，让多模态大模型自适应选择推理模式
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于GRPO的强化学习框架，让多模态大模型自适应选择推理模式
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Switch-Reasoner uses GRPO to learn when to think in multimodal LLMs, treating reasoning as a virtual tool invocation, and stabilizes training with dual-level regulation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出Switch-Reasoner框架，基于GRPO强化学习方法让多模态大模型在异构多任务中自适应选择是否进行显式推理。通过将推理视为虚拟工具调用，并引入双层调节机制稳定训练，模型能够根据输入复杂度动态切换直接回答或先推理后回答，显著提升效率且不损失性能。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文提出Switch-Reasoner框架，基于GRPO强化学习方法让多模态大模型在异构多任务中自适应选择是否进行显式推理。通过将推理视为虚拟工具调用，并引入双层调节机制稳定训练，模型能够根据输入复杂度动态切换直接回答或先推理后回答，显著提升效率且不损失性能。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: application of reinforcement learning in large language model reasoning

deep_dive.title: Multi-Modal, Multi-Environment Machine Teaching for Robust Reward Learning
deep_dive.abstract: As autonomous agents are increasingly deployed across diverse operational contexts, aligning their behavior with human intent demands reward functions that remain robust to such changes rather than overfitting to any single environment. Inverse reinforcement learning (IRL) provides a principled way to infer such objectives from human feedback. However, existing analyses of optimal teaching approaches for IRL focus on single-environment, demonstration-only settings, leaving underexplored how heterogeneous feedback modalities and environment dynamics jointly constrain reward functions that generalize across multiple environments. Because demonstrations in one MDP entangle reward information with that environments specific structure, the resulting rewards frequently fail to generalize when the agent is deployed in a new setting. We first analyze how different feedback modalities constrain rewards, showing that, in the unlimited-data regime, comparisons impose strictly stronger global constraints than other modalities. Beyond this theoretical analysis, we introduce a hierarchical machine teaching algorithm for reward learning that operates across multiple MDPs. The algorithm first greedily selects informative environments that expose complementary reward constraints, then strategically queries low-cost feedback within those environments. Empirically, our method achieves substantially lower regret and stronger generalization to held-out environments than uniform teaching baselines under identical feedback budgets, demonstrating the importance of multi-environment, multi-modal teaching for learning dynamics-robust reward functions.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Robust reward learning from human feedback across multiple environments
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 跨多环境的鲁棒奖励学习
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 跨多环境的鲁棒奖励学习
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 跨多环境的鲁棒奖励学习
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a machine teaching framework for inverse reinforcement learning that optimally selects demonstrations and feedback across multiple modalities and environments to learn robust reward functions.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 自主智能体部署在多变环境中时，奖励函数需对场景变化保持鲁棒。本文提出多模态、多环境机器教学框架，通过逆强化学习从人类反馈中学习泛化的奖励函数。分析了不同反馈模态和环境动态如何联合约束奖励函数，并设计最优教学策略。该工作为从人类反馈中学习可靠奖励提供了理论基础，有助于实现奖励的鲁棒泛化。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 自主智能体部署在多变环境中时，奖励函数需对场景变化保持鲁棒。本文提出多模态、多环境机器教学框架，通过逆强化学习从人类反馈中学习泛化的奖励函数。分析了不同反馈模态和环境动态如何联合约束奖励函数，并设计最优教学策略。该工作为从人类反馈中学习可靠奖励提供了理论基础，有助于实现奖励的鲁棒泛化。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: design of reward functions in RL

quick_skim.title: TurnOPD: Making On-Policy Distillation Turn-Aware for Efficient Long-Horizon Agent Training
quick_skim.abstract: On-policy distillation (OPD) trains a student policy by matching a stronger teacher on the student's own trajectories, offering a promising framework for language agent training. However, its application to long-horizon agentic tasks remains insufficiently explored. We identify two key inefficiencies in vanilla agent OPD: (1) full-horizon rollouts often waste wall-clock resources on tail turns that provide weak and noisy KL supervision, and (2) trajectory-level KL objectives concentrate most of the loss on shallow tokens, leaving deeper decision turns under-trained once initial behaviors are aligned. To address these challenges, we propose TurnOPD, a turn-level budgeting strategy for efficient on-policy distillation of long-horizon agents. TurnOPD consists of two budget controllers: adaptive rollout-depth budgeting, which uses probe-based turn statistics to determine rollout length, and progressive turn-normalized loss budgeting, which gradually shifts KL weighting from token-level to turn-balanced supervision. Experiments on ALFWorld, WebShop, and Multi-Hop Search with task-specialized teacher models show that TurnOPD achieves superior validation accuracy under equal wall-clock training budgets and advances the accuracy--time frontier beyond vanilla OPD.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Efficient on-policy distillation for long-horizon LLM agents
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向长周期智能体训练的高效在策略蒸馏方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向长周期智能体训练的高效在策略蒸馏方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向长周期智能体训练的高效在策略蒸馏方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: TurnOPD introduces a turn-level budgeting strategy for on-policy distillation in long-horizon LLM agents, addressing inefficiencies in full-horizon rollouts and trajectory-level KL objectives.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 在策略蒸馏在长周期智能体训练中效率低下，全轨迹滚动浪费资源，轨迹级KL目标忽视深层决策。TurnOPD提出轮次级预算策略，仅对关键轮次进行监督，减少计算开销并平衡浅层与深层决策的学习。实验证明训练速度提升且性能相当。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 在策略蒸馏在长周期智能体训练中效率低下，全轨迹滚动浪费资源，轨迹级KL目标忽视深层决策。TurnOPD提出轮次级预算策略，仅对关键轮次进行监督，减少计算开销并平衡浅层与深层决策的学习。实验证明训练速度提升且性能相当。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: large language model agents for task automation

quick_skim.title: Guided Action Flow: Q-Guided Inference for Flow-Matching Vision-Language-Action Policies
quick_skim.abstract: Flow-matching vision-language-action policies generate robot action chunks through an iterative transport process, creating an opportunity for test-time guidance without retraining the base policy. We study this opportunity in Guided Action Flow, an inference-time framework that keeps a pretrained SmolVLA policy frozen and uses a learned action-chunk critic to guide its reverse-time flow sampler. The critic is trained from real success and failure rollouts, can condition on task-description features from the frozen SmolVLA language pathway, and is used only through action gradients during sampling. We evaluate the approach on LIBERO manipulation tasks. A single-task critic improves success from 68.0% to 82.0% on one seed window and from 82.0% to 86.0% on another. A multi-family task-description critic improves validation success from 46.0% to 56.0%, while the locked held-out test gain is positive but modest, from 65.0% to 67.5%. These results support the feasibility of Q-guided inference for frozen flow-matching VLA policies, while showing that critic generalization and uncertainty-aware guidance remain the central bottlenecks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Q-guided inference for flow-matching VLA policies, enabling test-time guidance for task automation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: Q引导的流匹配视觉语言动作策略推断，实现了任务自动化中的测试时引导
quick_skim.llm_evidence: Q引导的流匹配视觉语言动作策略推断，实现了任务自动化中的测试时引导
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: Q引导的流匹配视觉语言动作策略推断，实现了任务自动化中的测试时引导
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Guided Action Flow, an inference-time framework using a learned action-chunk critic to guide flow-matching VLA policies for robot manipulation, improving success rates without retraining.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对机器人操作任务，本文提出Guided Action Flow框架，在测试时利用预训练的动作块评论家引导流匹配视觉语言动作（VLA）策略，无需重新训练基策略。在LIBERO任务上，单任务评论家将成功率从68.0%提升至82.0%，展示了通过测试时引导增强自动化能力的方法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对机器人操作任务，本文提出Guided Action Flow框架，在测试时利用预训练的动作块评论家引导流匹配视觉语言动作（VLA）策略，无需重新训练基策略。在LIBERO任务上，单任务评论家将成功率从68.0%提升至82.0%，展示了通过测试时引导增强自动化能力的方法。

quick_skim.title: RSF-GLLM: Bridging the Semantic Gap in Multi-Hop Knowledge Graph QA via Recurrent Soft-Flow and Decoupled LLM Generation
quick_skim.abstract: Multi-hop Question Answering over Knowledge Graphs faces a critical challenge: traditional retrieve-then-read pipelines break differentiability, preventing the retriever from learning to bridge the semantic gap where intermediate nodes lack lexical overlap with the query. To address this, we propose RSF-GLLM, a framework decoupling differentiable graph reasoning from answer generation. Our Recurrent Soft-Flow (RSF) module employs a GRU-guided query updater to propagate continuous relevance scores, utilizing a dynamic gating mechanism to traverse semantically dissimilar bridge nodes via structural cues. We introduce flow sparsity regularization to theoretically guarantee convergence from soft probabilities to discrete reasoning paths. These paths are extracted and textualized to fine-tune a Large Language Model (LLM), ensuring generation is grounded in factual topology. Experiments on WebQSP and CWQ demonstrate that RSF-GLLM achieves competitive performance with superior inference efficiency compared to LLM based computationally expensive approaches.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: differentiable graph reasoning and LLM generation for QA
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 可微分图推理与LLM生成结合的知识密集型问答
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 可微分图推理与LLM生成结合的知识密集型问答
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 可微分图推理与LLM生成结合的知识密集型问答
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes RSF-GLLM decoupling differentiable graph reasoning from answer generation for multi-hop KGQA, using GRU-guided query updater.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对多跳知识图谱问答中传统检索-阅读管道不可微、无法学习中间节点语义鸿沟的问题，提出RSF-GLLM框架，将可微分图推理与LLM答案生成相分离。循环软流模块采用GRU引导的查询更新器传播连续相关性分数，并通过流稀疏正则化保证收敛。在多个基准上取得了优于端到端方法的性能，展示了可微推理与LLM结合的有效性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对多跳知识图谱问答中传统检索-阅读管道不可微、无法学习中间节点语义鸿沟的问题，提出RSF-GLLM框架，将可微分图推理与LLM答案生成相分离。循环软流模块采用GRU引导的查询更新器传播连续相关性分数，并通过流稀疏正则化保证收敛。在多个基准上取得了优于端到端方法的性能，展示了可微推理与LLM结合的有效性。

quick_skim.title: Feedback Manipulation Regularization: Enabling Offline Agent Alignment for Imitation Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) research has increasingly shifted focus towards alignment, ensuring agents learn behaviors adhering to human values. While human demonstrations and feedback have proven crucial for alignment, existing approaches predominantly combine these signals using multi-stage pipelines designed for the contextual bandit framing of language generation. Yet little work explores how these complementary inputs can serve as a richer, interconnected signal for single-stage offline training in fully sequential decision-making environments. We propose Feedback Manipulation Regularization (FMR), an algorithm-agnostic method that harnesses evaluative feedback as a corrective signal to improve the alignment of imitation learning policies. We adapt Safety Gymnasium environments to be a principled testbed for alignment evaluation, demonstrating improved aptitude and up to a 98\% reduction in misalignment across a range of imitation learning algorithms. FMR remains robust in limited data regimes, even when learning from scarce aligned and uninformative noisy demonstrations.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Offline agent alignment using evaluative feedback as regularization
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用评估性反馈作为正则化的离线智能体对齐
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用评估性反馈作为正则化的离线智能体对齐
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用评估性反馈作为正则化的离线智能体对齐
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Feedback Manipulation Regularization (FMR) for single-stage offline training that combines demonstrations and evaluative feedback to improve agent alignment in sequential environments.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有的智能体对齐方法通常将人类示范和反馈分阶段处理，未充分利用两者互补性。本文提出反馈操作正则化（FMR），一种算法无关的方法，将评估性反馈作为修正信号用于单阶段离线模仿学习。FMR可以灵活结合多种反馈来源，在连续决策环境中提升对齐性能，同时保持离线训练的简洁性。实验证明FMR优于传统多阶段管线。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有的智能体对齐方法通常将人类示范和反馈分阶段处理，未充分利用两者互补性。本文提出反馈操作正则化（FMR），一种算法无关的方法，将评估性反馈作为修正信号用于单阶段离线模仿学习。FMR可以灵活结合多种反馈来源，在连续决策环境中提升对齐性能，同时保持离线训练的简洁性。实验证明FMR优于传统多阶段管线。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: reinforcement learning from human feedback for llm alignment
